prompt: Isometric three-quarter view of a workshop bench with two distinct stacks of countertop samples placed side by side for comparison. Left stack: thinner laminate counter samples in builder-grade beige speckle and faux-granite patterns, edges showing the brown particle-board substrate. Right stack: thicker quartz and engineered-stone slabs in soft greige with subtle veining and a polished mitered edge profile. Behind them, a roll of blue painter's tape, a steel framing square, a Festool plunge cut tracksaw out of focus. Shot with a 35mm prime lens at three-quarter angle, dramatic single overhead work-light pool casting falloff into the workshop background, mood is contemplative and decision-making. Slightly desaturated palette, brand red on a Milwaukee speed square as the only saturated color. NOT HGTV bright, working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text, no logos.
